steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The text is enumerating steps: "First, thoroughly research the question." then the blank, then "Third, develop an experiment." The blank introduces step two.

steps.title: Pick the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the sentences is sequence. The passage lists three numbered steps (First. Second. Third). The blank introduces the middle step and must continue the ordinal sequence.
  • Choice A — For example,: signals that a specific instance of a general claim follows. No general claim precedes the blank — the passage is listing steps, not making a claim. ✗
  • Choice B — Second,: signals the second item in an ordered sequence, fitting perfectly between "First" and "Third." ✓
  • Choice C — Therefore,: signals cause-effect — the blank sentence would be a logical result of researching the question. That's not the relationship; each step is independent. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead,: signals that the blank sentence replaces or contradicts the prior one. No contradiction exists here. ✗
That matches Choice B — Second,.
